evidence: Multiple compatibility files are pure pass-through wrappers with no added behavior: `src/tools/edit-video/hooks/useEditVideoSettings.ts`, `src/tools/image-generation/components/ImageGenerationForm/index.tsx`, `src/tools/image-generation/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts`, `src/tools/join-clips/settings.ts`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/sections/generation/index.ts`.

Migration intent is encoded as comments but not enforced in code paths: `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/legacyMigrations/legacyProjectImageSettings.ts` includes a remove-by date (`2026-05-31`) and deprecated fields, while wrappers remain open-ended for backward compatibility.

Several small modules have large explanatory/commentary blocks relative to trivial logic, a common generated-style pattern that lowers signal: `src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts`, `src/shared/lib/typeGuards.ts`, `src/shared/realtime/index.ts`, `src/shared/types/videoTrim.ts`, `src/shared/types/presetMetadata.ts`, `src/shared/types/steerableMotion.ts`.

findings.summary: Compatibility wrappers are mostly pass-through re-exports with no enforced migration endpoint.
evidence: `useEditVideoSettings.ts` only re-exports from shared with a compatibility comment.
`join-clips/settings.ts` is a direct re-export shim with no deprecation mechanism or usage guard.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a bounded migration plan in code: mark shim exports with `@deprecated` including a concrete removal version/date, add lint rules (`no-restricted-imports`) to block new imports from legacy paths, and migrate callers so these wrappers can be deleted in one tracked cleanup.

findings.summary: Large restating comment blocks around simple code create generated-style noise.
evidence: `useUpdatingTimestamp.ts` contains long docstrings/examples for a thin wrapper around `useLiveRelativeTimestamp`.
`typeGuards.ts` uses banner sections and verbose explanatory comments for straightforward checks.
`realtime/index.ts` has architecture narration while the module is primarily exports.
`videoTrim.ts` documents basic interface fields and return types extensively.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to non-obvious intent/constraints only; remove repetitive prose that restates names/types; keep short module-level rationale where needed and rely on clear symbol names/types for obvious behavior.

findings.summary: Migration sunset metadata exists only in comments, so legacy paths can persist indefinitely.
evidence: `legacyProjectImageSettings.ts` declares a sunset contract with `Remove by: 2026-05-31` and deprecated fields.
Downstream compatibility shims continue to expose old import paths with no telemetry, runtime warning, or CI check tied to the sunset date.
findings.suggestion: Add enforceable controls: track legacy-field reads via metrics/logging, fail CI on new legacy-path imports, and add a date-gated cleanup issue/check that blocks release once the sunset date passes until legacy usage is removed.
